output.final_deliverable:
Missing evidence: the request requires a web exploration starting from a specific climate-change news article and then following its outbound links to (1) a scientific paper/study, (2) a government policy document, and (3) an organization’s position statement, with URLs, summaries, relationship to the article, and a documented path. However, the provided `merged` results array is empty, and no search/visit outputs (URLs, page contents, or link paths) were provided to use as evidence. 

To complete this properly, I need (in merged results) at least:
- The starting news article URL and its content excerpt (or the visited-page output).
- The followed-link URLs and visited-page outputs for:
  1) the research paper/study,
  2) the government policy document,
  3) the organization’s position statement,
- The intermediate link trail showing how each destination was reached from the original article.

If you provide merged search/visit outputs (or rerun the workflow to populate `merged`), I can synthesize the requested deliverable with inline URL citations based strictly on those visited sources.
output.action_summary: Cannot produce the exploration because no search/visit evidence was provided (merged results are empty). Need the starting article plus visited pages for the paper, policy document, and position statement, including the link path from the article.

results.title: Position Statement on Climate Change | AGU
results.description: Global average temperatures will only stabilize after CO2 emissions are matched by the amount removed (net-zero) and emissions decline of non-CO2 climate pollutants.viii Limiting the overall increase in average temperature to 1.5°C above pre-industrial levels, the aspirational target set in ...

results.title: Climate | Department of Energy
results.description: Official websites use .gov A .gov website belongs to an official government organization in the United States. Secure .gov websites use HTTPS A lock ( ) or https:// means you’ve safely connected to the .gov website. Share sensitive information only on official, secure websites. On July 29, 2025, the Department of Energy (DOE) published a report entitled A Critical Review of Impacts of Greenhouse Gas Emissions on the U.S. Climate, evaluating existing peer-reviewed literature and government data on climate impacts of Greenhouse Gas (GHG) Emissions and providing a critical assessment of the conventional narrative on climate change.

results.title: How a major DOE report hides the whole truth on climate change
results.description: The DOE report relies on studies or reports that have sometimes been amplified by the fossil fuel industry, or are connected to conservative groups that oppose government efforts to address climate change. For example, the report cites a blog post that DOE report author Roy Spencer wrote about “faulty” climate models. He wrote the post for the Heritage Foundation, the same organization that produced the Project 2025 playbook that called for jettisoning climate policy and defunding science.

results.title: Climate Change draft position statement | AGU
results.description: I think the final statement would be strengthened if it said “Collective actions across all sectors…” and split into two sentences, with some word change suggestions: “Collective actions across all sectors to mitigate and adapt to the climate crisis are urgently needed to protect life on Earth. If truly transformative and just, these actions can yield significant benefits across current and future generations.”" ... results.title: Climate Change - Government Sources by Subject - Library Guides at University of Washington Libraries
results.description: Interactive climate change site designed by fourteen federal government agencies, centered on case studies, tools, expertise, regions, and topics. National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration (NOAA) | Climate · Maps, data, news, and features about climate change. National Aeronautics and Space Administration (NASA) | Global Climate Change · Facts, articles, solutions, data, and other resources with respect to climate change.

results.title: Internet Resources on Climate Change | U.S. Department of the Interior
results.description: The Environmental and Energy Study Institute (EESI) is a non-profit organization founded in 1984 by a bipartisan Congressional caucus dedicated to finding innovative environmental and energy solutions. Global Change Magazine The magazine of the International Geosphere-Biosphere Programme (IGBP), Global Change highlights its research and includes articles that link science, society and policy.

results.title: Full article: Researching climate policy in uncertain times
results.description: For climate governance watchers, statements emerging over the year from the G-20, regional groupings like ASEAN and the EU, and mutual interest groupings like BRICS will provide signals on the scope for future cooperation. Also important is understanding whether and how international organizations – notably the World Bank but also regional development banks and emergent bodies such as the Asia Infrastructure Investment Bank (Zeng, Citation2024) – direct funds and analysis toward climate and climate-relevant outcomes.
